title: Trump Says Iran’s Response to Latest U.S. Proposal ‘Totally Unacceptable’
summary: The United States and Iran have been discussing a 30-day extension to their cease-fire and a reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Trump Administration Is Considering Pausing the Federal Gas Tax to Lower Prices, Energy Secretary Says
summary: The tax is a little over 18 cents a gallon for gasoline. With the average national price above $4.50, the measure will provide little relief.

title: Oil Prices Rise as Prospects for U.S.-Iran Peace Deal Fizzle
summary: Oil prices rose and stock futures ticked down as investors reacted after the two sides failed to agree on a U.S.-Iran peace deal.

title: After Virginia Redistricting Map Is Tossed, Democrats Search Desperately for a Response
summary: A conversation involving House members from Virginia and the top House Democrat reflected the fury and desperation that has gripped the party after Friday’s ruling in the state.

title: Fearing an All-Republican Governor’s Race, Democrats Push for New Primary Rules in California
summary: The prospect of Democrats getting shut out of the general election for governor has spurred an effort to eliminate the state’s “top-two” open primary approach.

title: How Nebraska’s ‘Blue Dot’ Roiled a Democratic House Primary
summary: A speck of blue on an otherwise red area in some presidential elections, the dot is now dividing candidates in a key midterm contest.

title: G.O.P. Fatigue in Iowa Strains the Republican Primary for Governor
summary: Five candidates are battling for the nomination in the June 2 primary. The winner will oppose Rob Sand, a Democrat, in the November election.

title: Vance or Rubio? Trump Muses on Successor as the ‘Kids’ Fill Bigger Roles.
summary: Vice President JD Vance and Secretary of State Marco Rubio are boosting their profiles, generating speculation about who’s lining up for the 2028 presidential nomination.

title: With Commissioner Under Pressure, F.D.A. Opens Door to Flavored Vapes
summary: Though illicit e-cigarettes have flooded in from China, the new policy could allow major tobacco companies to sell from prime shelf space at thousands of stores.

title: Consumers Lean on a ‘Hamster Wheel’ of Credit to Manage Rising Costs
summary: As prices increase for gas, groceries and other staples, more and more households are borrowing to get by.

title: Can the Country’s ‘Dirtiest Hotel’ Get a Clean Slate?
summary: For decades, the Hotel Carter in Times Square was the site of squalor and crime. Now rundown and empty, its future is tied up in litigation but some hope for a rebirth.

title: Abraham Foxman, Warrior Against Antisemitism, Dies at 86
summary: For almost three decades, he led the Anti-Defamation League, winning access to presidents and prime ministers and meeting with Pope John Paul II.

title: Sales Are Up. Celebrities Are In. Is Gap Officially Back?
summary: Richard Dickson has drawn inspiration from the clothing retailer’s early days as he tries to regain its cultural cachet.

title: Beware of Wolves, but Straw Houses Could Help With Climate Change
summary: Near Hudson, N.Y., a Princeton University team erected a cottage made from the agricultural byproduct, which they said is more sustainable than bricks or concrete.

title: A Quietly Radical Experiment in Criminal Justice
summary: Hawaii might have the answer.

title: We’re at the Dawn of the Ozempic Era — and It’s Really Weird
summary: One in eight American adults is taking GLP-1s. What does that mean for how we relate to our food, bodies and one another?

title: Thailand’s Thaksin Shinawatra Is Out of Prison. What Will He Do Now?
summary: Mr. Thaksin has long been a foil to Thailand’s conservative establishment. He still holds significant influence, with his political party a member of the governing coalition.

title: 6 Bodies Found in a Boxcar in Texas, Officials Say
summary: The bodies were discovered in the city of Laredo. The circumstances of their deaths were not immediately known, the authorities said.

title: Hiker Killed by Bear at Glacier National Park, Officials Say
summary: The victim was a seasoned outdoorsman who “had no problem being alone in the woods,” his father said. He was found with injuries “consistent with those sustained by a bear encounter,” the National Park Service said.

title: Cruise Ship Linked to Hantavirus Outbreak Arrives in Spain’s Canary Islands for Disembarking
summary: Small groups of passengers and crew left the ship after it anchored off Spain’s Canary Islands on Sunday. Those aboard were expected to be flown to their home countries, officials said.
